Sexual Reproduction
requires fertilization of egg by sperm
monoecious vs dioecious species

Sexual Reproduction
requires fertilization of egg by sperm
Monoecious species
Every individual can, at some point in its life, produce both eggs and sperm.
Dioecious species
Any given individual is either male, and can only ever produce sperm, or is
female and can only ever produce eggs.
Monoecious species may be able to self-fertilize, may not
Note that distinction is in regard to what an INDIVIDUAL can do, not what the
species as a whole can or can’t do

Internal vs external fertilization
Internal fertilization
better probability of fertilization
fewer “random” sperm
stable environment
reduced egg predation

External fertilization
potentially larger numbers of offspring
more genetic variability
1 female x many males
Internal vs external fertilization
Broadcast spawning

Oviparous species lay eggs
development occurs outside the female
inside egg shell if in terrestrial environment

Ovoviviparous species retain eggs
development occurs inside egg, in female reproductive tract
no direct support from female

Viviparous species support embryo development
direct connection between female and embryo
Marsupials
Eutherians

Amniotic egg adapted for vivipary , placenta formation
Marsupials
Eutherians
Reptiles, including birds
Monotremes

No mixing of maternal and fetal blood
diffusion through fetal capillary wall only
